The genus Echinosepala is closely related to the genus Pleurothallis, and the two are sometimes considered to be the same genus. Echinosepala pan is classified as a type of orchid that grows on other plants, such as trees and bushes, using its roots. The classification of Echinosepala pan is within the broader context of the orchid family, which is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants.

intro: Echinosepala pan, also known as Echinosepala pan, is a type of orchid native to Southeast Asia, specifically the tropical regions of Thailand and Myanmar. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Echinosepala pan include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Echinosepala pan is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is commonly used to make cough syrups, expectorants, and respiratory tonics.
